3	use uv_test::uv_snapshot;
4	
5	/// Test that `cache size` returns 0 for an empty cache directory (raw output).
6	#[test]
7	fn cache_size_empty_raw() {
8	    let context = uv_test::test_context!("3.12");
9	
10	    // Clean cache first to ensure truly empty state
11	    context.clean().assert().success();
12	
13	    uv_snapshot!(context.cache_size().arg("--preview"), @"
14	    success: true
15	    exit_code: 0
16	    ----- stdout -----
17	    0
18	
19	    ----- stderr -----
20	    ");
21	}
22	
23	/// Test that `cache size` returns raw bytes after installing packages.
24	#[test]
25	fn cache_size_with_packages_raw() {
26	    let context = uv_test::test_context!("3.12");
27	
28	    // Install a requirement to populate the cache.
29	    context.pip_install().arg("iniconfig").assert().success();
30	
31	    // Check cache size is now positive (raw bytes).
32	    uv_snapshot!(context.with_filtered_cache_size().filters(), context.cache_size().arg("--preview"), @"
33	    success: true
34	    exit_code: 0
35	    ----- stdout -----
36	    [SIZE]
37	
38	    ----- stderr -----
39	    ");
40	}
41	
42	/// Test that `cache size --human` returns human-readable format after installing packages.
43	#[test]
44	fn cache_size_with_packages_human() {
45	    let context = uv_test::test_context!("3.12");
46	
47	    // Install a requirement to populate the cache.
48	    context.pip_install().arg("iniconfig").assert().success();
49	
50	    // Check cache size with --human flag
51	    uv_snapshot!(context.with_filtered_cache_size().filters(), context.cache_size().arg("--preview").arg("--human"), @"
52	    success: true
53	    exit_code: 0
54	    ----- stdout -----
55	    [SIZE]
56	
57	    ----- stderr -----
58	    ");
59	}

931	/// The different kinds of data in the cache are stored in different bucket, which in our case
932	/// are subdirectories of the cache root.
933	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Eq, PartialEq, Hash)]
934	pub enum CacheBucket {
935	    /// Wheels (excluding built wheels), alongside their metadata and cache policy.
936	    ///
937	    /// There are three kinds from cache entries: Wheel metadata and policy as `MsgPack` files, the
938	    /// wheels themselves, and the unzipped wheel archives. If a wheel file is over an in-memory
939	    /// size threshold, we first download the zip file into the cache, then unzip it into a
940	    /// directory with the same name (exclusive of the `.whl` extension).
941	    ///
942	    /// Cache structure:
943	    ///  * `wheel-metadata-v0/pypi/foo/{foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl}`
944	    ///  * `wheel-metadata-v0/<digest(index-url)>/foo/{foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl}`
945	    ///  * `wheel-metadata-v0/url/<digest(url)>/foo/{foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl}`
946	    ///
947	    /// See `uv_client::RegistryClient::wheel_metadata` for information on how wheel metadata
948	    /// is fetched.
949	    ///
950	    /// # Example
951	    ///
952	    /// Consider the following `requirements.in`:
953	    /// ```text
954	    /// # pypi wheel
955	    /// pandas
956	    /// # url wheel
957	    /// flask @ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/36/42/015c23096649b908c809c69388a805a571a3bea44362fe87e33fc3afa01f/flask-3.0.0-py3-none-any.whl
958	    /// ```
959	    ///
960	    /// When we run `pip compile`, it will only fetch and cache the metadata (and cache policy), it
961	    /// doesn't need the actual wheels yet:
962	    /// ```text
963	    /// wheel-v0
964	    /// ├── pypi
965	    /// │   ...
966	    /// │   ├── pandas
967	    /// │   │   └── pandas-2.1.3-cp310-cp310-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.msgpack
968	    /// │   ...
969	    /// └── url
970	    ///     └── 4b8be67c801a7ecb
971	    ///         └── flask
972	    ///             └── flask-3.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack
973	    /// ```
974	    ///
975	    /// We get the following `requirement.txt` from `pip compile`:
976	    ///
977	    /// ```text
978	    /// [...]
979	    /// flask @ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/36/42/015c23096649b908c809c69388a805a571a3bea44362fe87e33fc3afa01f/flask-3.0.0-py3-none-any.whl
980	    /// [...]
981	    /// pandas==2.1.3
982	    /// [...]
983	    /// ```
984	    ///
985	    /// If we run `pip sync` on `requirements.txt` on a different machine, it also fetches the
986	    /// wheels:
987	    ///
988	    /// TODO(konstin): This is still wrong, we need to store the cache policy too!
989	    /// ```text
990	    /// wheel-v0
991	    /// ├── pypi
992	    /// │   ...
993	    /// │   ├── pandas
994	    /// │   │   ├── pandas-2.1.3-cp310-cp310-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.whl
995	    /// │   │   ├── pandas-2.1.3-cp310-cp310-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64
996	    /// │   ...
997	    /// └── url
998	    ///     └── 4b8be67c801a7ecb
999	    ///         └── flask
1000	    ///             └── flask-3.0.0-py3-none-any.whl
1001	    ///                 ├── flask
1002	    ///                 │   └── ...
1003	    ///                 └── flask-3.0.0.dist-info
1004	    ///                     └── ...
1005	    /// ```
1006	    ///
1007	    /// If we run first `pip compile` and then `pip sync` on the same machine, we get both:
1008	    ///
1009	    /// ```text
1010	    /// wheels-v0
1011	    /// ├── pypi
1012	    /// │   ├── ...
1013	    /// │   ├── pandas
1014	    /// │   │   ├── pandas-2.1.3-cp312-cp312-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.msgpack
1015	    /// │   │   ├── pandas-2.1.3-cp312-cp312-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.whl
1016	    /// │   │   └── pandas-2.1.3-cp312-cp312-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64
1017	    /// │   │       ├── pandas
1018	    /// │   │       │   ├── ...
1019	    /// │   │       ├── pandas-2.1.3.dist-info
1020	    /// │   │       │   ├── ...
1021	    /// │   │       └── pandas.libs
1022	    /// │   ├── ...
1023	    /// └── url
1024	    ///     └── 4b8be67c801a7ecb
1025	    ///         └── flask
1026	    ///             ├── flask-3.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack
1027	    ///             ├── flask-3.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack
1028	    ///             └── flask-3.0.0-py3-none-any
1029	    ///                 ├── flask
1030	    ///                 │   └── ...
1031	    ///                 └── flask-3.0.0.dist-info
1032	    ///                     └── ...
1033	    Wheels,
1034	    /// Source distributions, wheels built from source distributions, their extracted metadata, and the
1035	    /// cache policy of the source distribution.
1036	    ///
1037	    /// The structure is similar of that of the `Wheel` bucket, except we have an additional layer
1038	    /// for the source distribution filename and the metadata is at the source distribution-level,
1039	    /// not at the wheel level.
1040	    ///
1041	    /// TODO(konstin): The cache policy should be on the source distribution level, the metadata we
1042	    /// can put next to the wheels as in the `Wheels` bucket.
1043	    ///
1044	    /// The unzipped source distribution is stored in a directory matching the source distribution
1045	    /// archive name.
1046	    ///
1047	    /// Source distributions are built into zipped wheel files (as PEP 517 specifies) and unzipped
1048	    /// lazily before installing. So when resolving, we only build the wheel and store the archive
1049	    /// file in the cache, when installing, we unpack it under the same name (exclusive of the
1050	    /// `.whl` extension). You may find a mix of wheel archive zip files and unzipped wheel
1051	    /// directories in the cache.
1052	    ///
1053	    /// Cache structure:
1054	    ///  * `built-wheels-v0/pypi/foo/34a17436ed1e9669/{manifest.msgpack, metadata.msgpack, foo-1.0.0.zip, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl, ...other wheels}`

1057	    ///  * `built-wheels-v0/git/<digest(url)>/<git sha>/foo/foo-1.0.0.zip/{metadata.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl, ...other wheels}`
1058	    ///
1059	    /// But the url filename does not need to be a valid source dist filename
1060	    /// (<https://github.com/search?q=path%3A**%2Frequirements.txt+master.zip&type=code>),
1061	    /// so it could also be the following and we have to take any string as filename:
1062	    ///  * `built-wheels-v0/url/<sha256(url)>/master.zip/metadata.msgpack`
1063	    ///
1064	    /// # Example
1065	    ///
1066	    /// The following requirements:

11	/// When the cache directory cannot be created (e.g., due to permissions), we should show a
12	/// chained error message that indicates we failed to initialize the cache.
13	#[test]
14	#[cfg(unix)]
15	fn cache_init_failure() -> Result<()> {
16	    use uv_test::ReadOnlyDirectoryGuard;
17	
18	    let context = uv_test::test_context!("3.12");
19	
20	    let pyproject_toml = context.temp_dir.child("pyproject.toml");
21	    pyproject_toml.write_str(
22	        r#"
23	        [project]
24	        name = "project"
25	        version = "0.1.0"
26	        requires-python = ">=3.12"
27	        dependencies = ["iniconfig"]
28	        "#,
29	    )?;
30	
31	    // Create a read-only directory that will serve as the parent of the cache.
32	    // The guard sets it to read-only and restores original permissions on drop (including panic).
33	    let cache_parent = context.temp_dir.child("cache_parent");
34	    fs_err::create_dir(&cache_parent)?;
35	    let _guard = ReadOnlyDirectoryGuard::new(cache_parent.path())?;
36	
37	    // Point the cache to a subdirectory within the read-only parent
38	    let cache_dir = cache_parent.child("cache");
39	
40	    // Filter both the relative path (in the first line) and absolute path (in the cause)
41	    let context = context
42	        .with_filter((r"cache_parent/cache", "[CACHE_DIR]"))
43	        .with_filter((
44	            r"failed to create directory `.*`",
45	            "failed to create directory `[CACHE_DIR]`",
46	        ));
47	
48	    // Build the sync command manually to use our custom cache directory.
49	    // We can't use context.sync() because it adds --cache-dir with the default cache.
50	    let mut command = Command::new(get_bin!());
51	    command
52	        .arg("sync")
53	        .arg("--cache-dir")
54	        .arg(cache_dir.path())
55	        .current_dir(context.temp_dir.path());
56	    context.add_shared_env(&mut command, false);
57	
58	    // Running a command should fail with a chained error about cache initialization
59	    uv_snapshot!(context.filters(), command, @"
60	    success: false
61	    exit_code: 2
62	    ----- stdout -----
63	
64	    ----- stderr -----
65	    error: Failed to initialize cache at `[CACHE_DIR]`
66	      Caused by: failed to create directory `[CACHE_DIR]`: Permission denied (os error 13)
67	    ");
68	
69	    Ok(())
70	}

7	/// Cache wheels and their metadata, both from remote wheels and built from source distributions.
8	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
9	pub enum WheelCache<'a> {
10	    /// Either PyPI or an alternative index, which we key by index URL.
11	    Index(&'a IndexUrl),
12	    /// A direct URL dependency, which we key by URL.
13	    Url(&'a DisplaySafeUrl),
14	    /// A path dependency, which we key by URL.
15	    Path(&'a DisplaySafeUrl),
16	    /// An editable dependency, which we key by URL.
17	    Editable(&'a DisplaySafeUrl),
18	    /// A Git dependency, which we key by URL (including LFS state), SHA.
19	    ///
20	    /// Note that this variant only exists for source distributions; wheels can't be delivered
21	    /// through Git.
22	    Git(&'a DisplaySafeUrl, &'a str),
23	}
24	
25	impl WheelCache<'_> {
26	    /// The root directory for a cache bucket.
27	    pub fn root(&self) -> PathBuf {
28	        match self {
29	            Self::Index(IndexUrl::Pypi(_)) => WheelCacheKind::Pypi.root(),
30	            Self::Index(url) => WheelCacheKind::Index
31	                .root()
32	                .join(cache_digest(&CanonicalUrl::new(url.url()))),
33	            Self::Url(url) => WheelCacheKind::Url
34	                .root()
35	                .join(cache_digest(&CanonicalUrl::new(url))),
36	            Self::Path(url) => WheelCacheKind::Path
37	                .root()
38	                .join(cache_digest(&CanonicalUrl::new(url))),
39	            Self::Editable(url) => WheelCacheKind::Editable
40	                .root()
41	                .join(cache_digest(&CanonicalUrl::new(url))),
42	            Self::Git(url, sha) => WheelCacheKind::Git
43	                .root()
44	                .join(cache_digest(&CanonicalUrl::new(url)))
45	                .join(sha),
46	        }
47	    }
48	
49	    /// A subdirectory in a bucket for wheels for a specific package.
50	    pub fn wheel_dir(&self, package_name: impl AsRef<Path>) -> PathBuf {
51	        self.root().join(package_name)
52	    }
53	}
54

56	pub(crate) enum WheelCacheKind {
57	    /// A cache of data from PyPI.
58	    Pypi,
59	    /// A cache of data from an alternative index.
60	    Index,
61	    /// A cache of data from an arbitrary URL.
62	    Url,
63	    /// A cache of data from a local path.
64	    Path,
65	    /// A cache of data from an editable URL.
66	    Editable,

69	}

76	            Self::Url => "url",
77	            Self::Path => "path",
78	            Self::Editable => "editable",
79	            Self::Git => "git",
80	        }
81	    }
82

84	        Path::new(self.to_str()).to_path_buf()
85	    }
86	}

934	pub enum CacheBucket {
935	    /// Wheels (excluding built wheels), alongside their metadata and cache policy.
936	    ///
937	    /// There are three kinds from cache entries: Wheel metadata and policy as `MsgPack` files, the
938	    /// wheels themselves, and the unzipped wheel archives. If a wheel file is over an in-memory
939	    /// size threshold, we first download the zip file into the cache, then unzip it into a
940	    /// directory with the same name (exclusive of the `.whl` extension).
941	    ///
942	    /// Cache structure:
943	    ///  * `wheel-metadata-v0/pypi/foo/{foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl}`
944	    ///  * `wheel-metadata-v0/<digest(index-url)>/foo/{foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl}`
945	    ///  * `wheel-metadata-v0/url/<digest(url)>/foo/{foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl}`
946	    ///
947	    /// See `uv_client::RegistryClient::wheel_metadata` for information on how wheel metadata
948	    /// is fetched.
949	    ///
950	    /// # Example
951	    ///
952	    /// Consider the following `requirements.in`:
953	    /// ```text
954	    /// # pypi wheel
955	    /// pandas
956	    /// # url wheel
957	    /// flask @ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/36/42/015c23096649b908c809c69388a805a571a3bea44362fe87e33fc3afa01f/flask-3.0.0-py3-none-any.whl
958	    /// ```
959	    ///
960	    /// When we run `pip compile`, it will only fetch and cache the metadata (and cache policy), it
961	    /// doesn't need the actual wheels yet:
962	    /// ```text
963	    /// wheel-v0
964	    /// ├── pypi

Excellent! Now I have all the information needed. Let me create a comprehensive report.

## Summary of uv Codebase Architecture

I've thoroughly explored the uv codebase and compiled the exact file paths and line numbers for all the key components you requested. Here's what I found:

### 1. CLI Subcommand Definition (CacheCommand enum)

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477504365-bipmwl/crates/uv-cli/src/lib.rs`

**Lines 878-905:** CacheCommand enum definition with Size variant

```
#[derive(Subcommand)]
pub enum CacheCommand {
    /// Clear the cache, removing all entries or those linked to specific packages.
    #[command(alias = "clear")]
    Clean(CleanArgs),
    /// Prune all unreachable objects from the cache.
    Prune(PruneArgs),
    /// Show the cache directory.
    Dir,
    /// Show the cache size.
    Size(SizeArgs),  // <-- Lines 899-904
}
```

**Lines 947-952:** SizeArgs struct defining the `--human` argument

```
#[derive(Args, Debug)]
pub struct SizeArgs {
    /// Display the cache size in human-readable format (e.g., `1.2 GiB` instead of raw bytes).
    #[arg(long = "human", short = 'H', alias = "human-readable")]
    pub human: bool,
}
```

**Lines 548-553:** Cache command registered in Commands enum

### 2. Human-Readable Byte Size Formatting

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477504365-bipmwl/crates/uv-console/src/lib.rs`

**Lines 300-314:** `human_readable_bytes` function

```rust
/// Formats a number of bytes into a human readable SI-prefixed size (binary units).
/// Returns a tuple of `(quantity, units)`.
pub fn human_readable_bytes(bytes: u64) -> (f32, &'static str) {
    const UNITS: [&str; 7] = ["B", "KiB", "MiB", "GiB", "TiB", "PiB", "EiB"];
    let bytes_f32 = bytes as f32;
    let i = ((bytes_f32.log2() / 10.0) as usize).min(UNITS.len() - 1);
    (bytes_f32 / 1024_f32.powi(i as i32), UNITS[i])
}
```

Uses binary units (KiB, MiB, GiB, etc.) with logarithmic scaling.

### 3. Current `uv cache size` Output Formatting

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477504365-bipmwl/crates/uv/src/commands/cache_size.rs`

**Lines 1-48:** Complete cache_size function implementation

Key formatting logic (lines 39-44):
```rust
if human_readable {
    let (bytes, unit) = human_readable_bytes(total_bytes);
    writeln!(printer.stdout_important(), "{bytes:.1}{unit}")?;
} else {
    writeln!(printer.stdout_important(), "{total_bytes}")?;
}
```

The function uses:
- `diskus::DiskUsage` crate to calculate directory sizes
- Outputs with `.1` precision (one decimal place) when using human-readable format
- Uses `printer.stdout_important()` for output
- Returns `ExitStatus::Success` or handles zero bytes case separately

### 4. Integration Test Patterns for Cache Commands

**Test File Structure:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477504365-bipmwl/crates/uv/tests/it/cache_size.rs` - Cache size tests
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477504365-bipmwl/crates/uv/tests/it/cache_clean.rs` - Clean tests
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477504365-bipmwl/crates/uv/tests/it/cache_prune.rs` - Prune tests
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477504365-bipmwl/crates/uv/tests/it/cache.rs` - General cache tests

**Test Registration:** Lines 18-27 of `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477504365-bipmwl/crates/uv/tests/it/main.rs`

**Test Patterns in cache_size.rs (lines 1-60):**

1. **Empty cache test** (lines 5-21): `cache_size_empty_raw()`
   - Uses `context.clean()` to ensure clean state
   - Calls `context.cache_size().arg("--preview")`
   - Uses `uv_snapshot!()` macro for assertion

2. **Package installation pattern** (lines 23-40): `cache_size_with_packages_raw()`
   - Installs requirement via `context.pip_install().arg("iniconfig")`
   - Uses `context.with_filtered_cache_size().filters()` for output filtering
   - Replaces numeric size with `[SIZE]` placeholder for consistent snapshots

3. **Human-readable format test** (lines 42-59): `cache_size_with_packages_human()`
   - Calls cache size with `arg("--preview").arg("--human")`
   - Same filtering pattern for snapshot testing

Common test infrastructure:
- `uv_test::test_context!("3.12")` creates isolated test environment
- `uv_snapshot!()` macro for snapshot testing
- Filtering mechanisms for deterministic output (replacing variable data with placeholders)
- Output captured from stdout using `printer.stdout_important()`

### 5. Command Wiring from CLI to Handlers

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477504365-bipmwl/crates/uv/src/lib.rs`

**Lines 1176-1181:** Command dispatch logic in main execution path

```rust
Commands::Cache(CacheNamespace {
    command: CacheCommand::Size(args),
}) => commands::cache_size(&cache, args.human, printer, globals.preview),
```

**Export in commands module:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477504365-bipmwl/crates/uv/src/commands/mod.rs`

**Lines 16-21:** Public re-export of cache functions
```rust
pub(crate) use cache_clean::cache_clean;
pub(crate) use cache_dir::cache_dir;
pub(crate) use cache_prune::cache_prune;
pub(crate) use cache_size::cache_size;
```

**Lines 65:** Re-export of human_readable_bytes utility
```rust
pub(crate) use uv_console::human_readable_bytes;
```

**Lines 81-84:** Module declarations for cache subcommands
```rust
mod cache_clean;
mod cache_dir;
mod cache_prune;
mod cache_size;
```

### Architecture Summary

The architecture follows this flow:
1. **CLI Definition** (uv-cli crate): `CacheCommand` enum with `Size(SizeArgs)` variant
2. **Argument Parsing** (clap framework): `--human` flag mapped to `SizeArgs.human` boolean
3. **Command Dispatch** (uv/lib.rs): Pattern match on `CacheCommand::Size` and call handler
4. **Handler Implementation** (cache_size.rs): Uses `human_readable_bytes()` utility for formatting
5. **Output**: Formats via `printer.stdout_important()` with either raw bytes or human-readable format
6. **Testing**: Integration tests with context objects, snapshot testing, and filtering utilities

Human size formatting uses binary units (KiB, MiB, GiB, TiB, PiB, EiB) with logarithmic scaling to automatically select the appropriate unit.

### 2. Human-Readable Byte Size Formatting

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477504365-bipmwl/crates/uv-console/src/lib.rs`

**Lines 300-314:** `human_readable_bytes` function

```rust
/// Formats a number of bytes into a human readable SI-prefixed size (binary units).
/// Returns a tuple of `(quantity, units)`.
pub fn human_readable_bytes(bytes: u64) -> (f32, &'static str) {
    const UNITS: [&str; 7] = ["B", "KiB", "MiB", "GiB", "TiB", "PiB", "EiB"];
    let bytes_f32 = bytes as f32;
    let i = ((bytes_f32.log2() / 10.0) as usize).min(UNITS.len() - 1);
    (bytes_f32 / 1024_f32.powi(i as i32), UNITS[i])
}
```

Uses binary units (KiB, MiB, GiB, etc.) with logarithmic scaling.

} else {

415	pub enum Commands {

417	    #[command(
418	        after_help = "Use `uv help auth` for more details.",

429	        after_help = "Use `uv help tool` for more details.",
430	        after_long_help = ""
431	    )]
432	    Tool(ToolNamespace),
433	
434	    /// Manage Python versions and installations
435	    ///
436	    /// Generally, uv first searches for Python in a virtual environment, either active or in a
437	    /// `.venv` directory in the current working directory or any parent directory. If a virtual
438	    /// environment is not required, uv will then search for a Python interpreter. Python
439	    /// interpreters are found by searching for Python executables in the `PATH` environment
440	    /// variable.
441	    ///
442	    /// On Windows, the registry is also searched for Python executables.
443	    ///
444	    /// By default, uv will download Python if a version cannot be found. This behavior can be
445	    /// disabled with the `--no-python-downloads` flag or the `python-downloads` setting.
446	    ///
447	    /// The `--python` option allows requesting a different interpreter.
448	    ///
449	    /// The following Python version request formats are supported:
450	    ///
451	    /// - `<version>` e.g. `3`, `3.12`, `3.12.3`
452	    /// - `<version-specifier>` e.g. `>=3.12,<3.13`
453	    /// - `<version><short-variant>` (e.g., `3.13t`, `3.12.0d`)
454	    /// - `<version>+<variant>` (e.g., `3.13+freethreaded`, `3.12.0+debug`)
455	    /// - `<implementation>` e.g. `cpython` or `cp`
456	    /// - `<implementation>@<version>` e.g. `cpython@3.12`
457	    /// - `<implementation><version>` e.g. `cpython3.12` or `cp312`
458	    /// - `<implementation><version-specifier>` e.g. `cpython>=3.12,<3.13`
459	    /// - `<implementation>-<version>-<os>-<arch>-<libc>` e.g. `cpython-3.12.3-macos-aarch64-none`
460	    ///
461	    /// Additionally, a specific system Python interpreter can often be requested with:
462	    ///
463	    /// - `<executable-path>` e.g. `/opt/homebrew/bin/python3`
464	    /// - `<executable-name>` e.g. `mypython3`
465	    /// - `<install-dir>` e.g. `/some/environment/`
466	    ///
467	    /// When the `--python` option is used, normal discovery rules apply but discovered interpreters
468	    /// are checked for compatibility with the request, e.g., if `pypy` is requested, uv will first
469	    /// check if the virtual environment contains a PyPy interpreter then check if each executable
470	    /// in the path is a PyPy interpreter.
471	    ///
472	    /// uv supports discovering CPython, PyPy, and GraalPy interpreters. Unsupported interpreters
473	    /// will be skipped during discovery. If an unsupported interpreter implementation is requested,
474	    /// uv will exit with an error.

476	    #[command(
477	        after_help = "Use `uv help python` for more details.",
478	        after_long_help = ""
479	    )]

489	    /// By default, creates a virtual environment named `.venv` in the working
490	    /// directory. An alternative path may be provided positionally.
491	    ///
492	    /// If in a project, the default environment name can be changed with
493	    /// the `UV_PROJECT_ENVIRONMENT` environment variable; this only applies
494	    /// when run from the project root directory.
495	    ///
496	    /// If a virtual environment exists at the target path, it will be removed
497	    /// and a new, empty virtual environment will be created.
498	    ///
499	    /// When using uv, the virtual environment does not need to be activated. uv
500	    /// will find a virtual environment (named `.venv`) in the working directory
501	    /// or any parent directories.

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct Cache {
    /// The cache directory.
    root: PathBuf,
    /// The refresh strategy to use when reading from the cache.
    refresh: Refresh,
    /// A temporary cache directory, if the user requested `--no-cache`.
    temp_dir: Option<Arc<tempfile::TempDir>>,
    /// Ensure that `uv cache` operations don't remove items from the cache that are used by another
    /// uv process.
    lock_file: Option<Arc<LockedFile>>,
}
```

**Key Cache Methods:**
- `bucket()` (line 281): Returns the path for a specific cache bucket
- `shard()` (line 286): Compute an entry in the cache
- `entry()` (line 291-298): Compute a specific entry in the cache
- `archive()` (line 301): Return path to an archive in the cache
- `prune()` (line 582): Run garbage collector on cache

```rust
pub struct Removal {
    /// The number of files removed.

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477504365-bipmwl/crates/uv-cache/src/cli.rs`

**CacheArgs Struct (lines 9-32):**
- `no_cache: bool` - Use temporary directory instead
- `cache_dir: Option<PathBuf>` - Custom cache directory path

**Cache Resolution Order (lines 43-78):**
1. Temporary cache if `--no-cache` requested
2. User-specified `--cache-dir` or `UV_CACHE_DIR`
3. Legacy cache directory (if exists, for backwards compatibility)
4. System-appropriate cache directory via `uv_dirs`
5. Fallback to `.uv_cache` in current directory

---

### 8. **CacheEntry and CacheShard Structures**
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477504365-bipmwl/crates/uv-cache/src/lib.rs`

**CacheEntry Struct (lines 51-105):**
- Wraps `PathBuf` for individual cache entries
- Methods: `lock()`, `path()`, `dir()`, `with_file()`, `into_path_buf()`, `shard()`

**CacheShard Struct (lines 114-158):**
- Wraps `PathBuf` for cache subdirectories
- Methods: `entry()`, `shard()`, `lock()`, `into_path_buf()`

---

### 9. **Key File Paths Summary**

| Component | Path |
|---|---|
| Main Cache Crate | `/crates/uv-cache/src/lib.rs` |
| Cache Library | `/crates/uv-cache/src/lib.rs` (1,380+ lines) |
| Wheel Cache Logic | `/crates/uv-cache/src/wheel.rs` (93 lines) |
| Removal & Size | `/crates/uv-cache/src/removal.rs` (259 lines) |
| Archive IDs | `/crates/uv-cache/src/archive.rs` (40 lines) |
| CLI Arguments | `/crates/uv-cache/src/cli.rs` (141 lines) |
| Cache Info | `/crates/uv-cache-info/src/cache_info.rs` (450 lines) |
| Timestamps | `/crates/uv-cache-info/src/timestamp.rs` (53 lines) |
| Cache Keys | `/crates/uv-cache-key/src/` |

---

### 10. **Dependencies**
Key crates used by uv-cache:
- `uv-fs` - File system utilities
- `walkdir` - Directory traversal for size calculations
- `tempfile` - Temporary directories
- `nanoid` - Unique ID generation for archives
- `rmp-serde` - MessagePack serialization
- `serde` - Serialization framework

This comprehensive structure allows uv to manage multiple cache buckets with atomic operations, symlink-based deduplication, and sophisticated size tracking for cache cleanup operations.

|---|---|
| Main Cache Crate | `/crates/uv-cache/src/lib.rs` |

- `uv-fs` - File system utilities

- `tempfile` - Temporary directories
- `nanoid` - Unique ID generation for archives
- `rmp-serde` - MessagePack serialization
- `serde` - Serialization framework

This comprehensive structure allows uv to manage multiple cache buckets with atomic operations, symlink-based deduplication, and sophisticated size tracking for cache cleanup operations.

### 5. Cache Size Calculation

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477504365-bipmwl/crates/uv/src/commands/cache_size.rs`

**Lines 13-47**:

```rust
pub(crate) fn cache_size(
    cache: &Cache,
    human_readable: bool,
    printer: Printer,
    preview: Preview,
) -> Result<ExitStatus> {
    // Check if cache exists
    if !cache.root().exists() {
        if human_readable {
            writeln!(printer.stdout_important(), "0B")?;
        } else {
            writeln!(printer.stdout_important(), "0")?;
        }
        return Ok(ExitStatus::Success);
    }

    // Use diskus crate to calculate total disk usage
    let disk_usage = DiskUsage::new(vec![cache.root().to_path_buf()]);
    let total_bytes = disk_usage.count_ignoring_errors();

    if human_readable {
        let (bytes, unit) = human_readable_bytes(total_bytes);
        writeln!(printer.stdout_important(), "{bytes:.1}{unit}")?;
    } else {
        writeln!(printer.stdout_important(), "{total_bytes}")?;
    }
    
    Ok(ExitStatus::Success)
}
```

**human_readable_bytes** function:
- Location: Re-exported from `uv_console` crate (line 6, commands/mod.rs line: `pub(crate) use uv_console::human_readable_bytes;`)
- Converts bytes to human-readable format (e.g., "1.2 GiB")
- Returns tuple of (bytes as f64, unit as &str)

**Removal struct** (lines 45-54 in removal.rs):
```rust
pub struct Removal {
    pub num_files: u64,
    pub num_dirs: u64,
    pub total_bytes: u64,  // Exact byte count, not block size
}
```

### 6. Display Flags & Output Patterns

**For `uv cache size`**:
- `--human` / `-H` / `--human-readable` - Output in human-readable format
- `--preview` flag - Required to suppress experimental warning
- Default output: Raw bytes (integer)
- Human output: e.g., "1.2 GiB"

**For `uv cache clean`**:
- `--force` - Skip lock and proceed immediately
- Output to stderr with: "Removed [N] files ([SIZE])" format
- Uses human_readable_bytes for size display

**For `uv cache prune`**:
- `--force` - Skip lock and proceed immediately
- `--ci` - Removes pre-built wheels but keeps wheels built from source
- Output to stderr

**For `uv cache dir`**:
- No flags beyond global options
- Outputs the cache directory path to stdout

**NOTE**: No `--json` flag currently exists for cache commands. Output is plain text only.

### 7. Command Handler Files

Located in `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477504365-bipmwl/crates/uv/src/commands/`:

| File | Size | Purpose |
|------|------|---------|
| `cache_size.rs` | 1.3 KB | `uv cache size` implementation |
| `cache_clean.rs` | 3.4 KB | `uv cache clean` implementation |
| `cache_prune.rs` | 2.9 KB | `uv cache prune` implementation |
| `cache_dir.rs` | 419 B | `uv cache dir` implementation |

### 8. Key Implementation Patterns

**Lock Acquisition Pattern** (used by clean and prune):
```rust
let cache = match cache.with_exclusive_lock_no_wait() {
    Ok(cache) => cache,
    Err(cache) if force => {
        debug!("Cache is currently in use, proceeding due to `--force`");
        cache
    }
    Err(cache) => {
        writeln!(printer.stderr(), "Cache is currently in-use, waiting...")?;
        cache.with_exclusive_lock().await?
    }
};
```

**Reporter Pattern** (for progress/statistics):
- `CleaningDirectoryReporter` - for full cache clear
- `CleaningPackageReporter` - for package-specific removal

### 9. Test Files

- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477504365-bipmwl/crates/uv/tests/it/cache_size.rs` - Tests for `uv cache size`
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477504365-bipmwl/crates/uv/tests/it/cache_clean.rs` - Tests for `uv cache clean`
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775477504365-bipmwl/crates/uv/tests/it/cache_prune.rs` - Tests for `uv cache prune`

This structure allows for modular command implementation while sharing the core Cache struct and bucket management logic.

888	    /// `%LOCALAPPDATA%\uv\cache` on Windows.
889	    ///
890	    /// When `--no-cache` is used, the cache is stored in a temporary directory and discarded when
891	    /// the process exits.

893	    /// An alternative cache directory may be specified via the `cache-dir` setting, the
894	    /// `--cache-dir` option, or the `$UV_CACHE_DIR` environment variable.
895	    ///
896	    /// Note that it is important for performance for the cache directory to be located on the same
897	    /// file system as the Python environment uv is operating on.

929	    /// Actions, it's faster to omit pre-built wheels from the cache and instead have re-download

244	            .push((r"(?m)^\d+\n".to_string(), "[SIZE]\n".to_string()));
245	        // Filter human-readable sizes (e.g., "384.2 KiB")
246	        self.filters.push((
247	            r"(?m)^\d+(\.\d+)? [KMGT]i?B\n".to_string(),
248	            "[SIZE]\n".to_string(),
249	        ));
250	        self
251	    }
252	
253	    /// Add extra standard filtering for Windows-compatible missing file errors.
254	    #[must_use]
255	    pub fn with_filtered_missing_file_error(mut self) -> Self {
256	        // The exact message string depends on the system language, so we remove it.
257	        // We want to only remove the phrase after `Caused by:`
258	        self.filters.push((
259	            r"[^:\n]* \(os error 2\)".to_string(),
260	            " [OS ERROR 2]".to_string(),
261	        ));
262	        // Replace the Windows "The system cannot find the path specified. (os error 3)"
263	        // with the Unix "No such file or directory (os error 2)"
264	        // and mask the language-dependent message.

1	# Plan: `uv cache stats` subcommand
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	`uv cache size` exists and shows the total size of the cache directory as a single number. Users want finer-grained visibility: how much space is consumed by each cache bucket (wheels, sdists, git, interpreter metadata, etc.), so they can make informed decisions about what to clean. The new `uv cache stats` subcommand shows a per-bucket breakdown with a total at the end, and supports `--human` for human-readable sizes — following the same patterns and conventions already established by `uv cache size`.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Files to Modify
10	
11	### 1. `crates/uv-preview/src/lib.rs`
12	
13	Add a new `CacheStats` preview feature flag (the next available bit after `ProjectDirectoryMustExist = 1 << 27`).
14

16

18	CacheStats = 1 << 28,
19	```
20	
21	**In `as_str()` match (line 201):**
22	
23	```rust
24	Self::CacheStats => "cache-stats",
25	```
26	
27	**In `FromStr` match (line 249):**
28	
29	```rust
30	"cache-stats" => Self::CacheStats,
31	```
32	
33	---
34

40	/// Show a per-bucket breakdown of cache usage.
41	///
42	/// Displays the size of each cache bucket (wheels, sdists, git, etc.) along with
43	/// a total. By default, outputs sizes in raw bytes; use `--human` for human-readable output.
44	Stats(StatsArgs),
45	```
46	
47	**New `StatsArgs` struct (after `SizeArgs` at line 952), mirroring `SizeArgs` exactly:**
48

51	pub struct StatsArgs {
52	    /// Display the cache sizes in human-readable format (e.g., `1.2 GiB` instead of raw bytes).
53	    #[arg(long = "human", short = 'H', alias = "human-readable")]
54	    pub human: bool,
55	}
56	```
57	
58	---
59	
60	### 3. `crates/uv/src/commands/mod.rs`
61	
62	**Add re-export (near line 21, alongside the other cache exports):**
63	
64	```rust

66	```
67	
68	**Add module declaration (near line 84, alongside the other `mod cache_*` entries):**
69

72	```
73

77	
78	**Add dispatch (after the `CacheCommand::Size` arm at line 1181):**
79	
80	```rust
81	Commands::Cache(CacheNamespace {
82	    command: CacheCommand::Stats(args),
83	}) => commands::cache_stats(&cache, args.human, printer, globals.preview),
84	```
85

89	
90	**Add module declaration (after `mod cache_size;` at line 27):**
91

94	mod cache_stats;
95	```
96	
97	---
98	
99	## Files to Create
100	
101	### 6. `crates/uv/src/commands/cache_stats.rs` (new file)
102	
103	```rust
104	use std::fmt::Write;
105	
106	use anyhow::Result;
107	use diskus::DiskUsage;
108	
109	use crate::commands::{ExitStatus, human_readable_bytes};
110	use crate::printer::Printer;
111	use uv_cache::{Cache, CacheBucket};
112	use uv_preview::{Preview, PreviewFeature};
113	use uv_warnings::warn_user;
114	
115	/// Display a per-bucket breakdown of the cache size.
116	pub(crate) fn cache_stats(
117	    cache: &Cache,
118	    human_readable: bool,
119	    printer: Printer,
120	    preview: Preview,
121	) -> Result<ExitStatus> {
122	    if !preview.is_enabled(PreviewFeature::CacheStats) {
123	        warn_user!(
124	            "`uv cache stats` is experimental and may change without warning. Pass `--preview-features {}` to disable this warning.",
125	            PreviewFeature::CacheStats
126	        );
127	    }
128	
129	    let mut total_bytes: u64 = 0;
130	
131	    for bucket in CacheBucket::iter() {
132	        let path = cache.bucket(bucket);
133	        let bucket_bytes = if path.exists() {
134	            DiskUsage::new(vec![path]).count_ignoring_errors()
135	        } else {
136	            0
137	        };
138	        total_bytes += bucket_bytes;
139	
140	        if human_readable {
141	            let (size, unit) = human_readable_bytes(bucket_bytes);
142	            writeln!(printer.stdout_important(), "{bucket:<20} {size:.1}{unit}")?;
143	        } else {
144	            writeln!(printer.stdout_important(), "{bucket:<20} {bucket_bytes}")?;
145	        }
146	    }
147	
148	    // Separator line and total row
149	    writeln!(printer.stdout_important(), "{:-<30}", "")?;
150	    if human_readable {
151	        let (size, unit) = human_readable_bytes(total_bytes);
152	        writeln!(printer.stdout_important(), "{:<20} {size:.1}{unit}", "Total")?;
153	    } else {
154	        writeln!(printer.stdout_important(), "{:<20} {total_bytes}", "Total")?;
155	    }
156	
157	    Ok(ExitStatus::Success)
158	}
159	```
160	
161	**Key implementation notes:**
162	
163	- Uses `CacheBucket::iter()` (defined at line 1316 of `uv-cache/src/lib.rs`) which covers all 10 active buckets: Wheels, SourceDistributions, FlatIndex, Git, Interpreter, Simple, Archive, Builds, Environments, Binaries. (Python bucket is not included in `iter()` — consistent with existing behavior.)
164	- `cache.bucket(bucket)` (line 281 of `uv-cache/src/lib.rs`) returns the full path to each bucket's directory.
165	- `CacheBucket` implements `Display` via `to_str()` (line 1334) giving versioned names like `wheels-v6`, `sdists-v9`, etc. — the same names as the actual directories.
166	- `diskus::DiskUsage` is already a dependency used by `cache_size.rs`; no new dependency needed.
167	- `human_readable_bytes` is re-exported from `uv_console` via `crate::commands` (line 65 of `commands/mod.rs`).
168	- Left-pads bucket name to 20 chars; `environments-v2` (15 chars) is the longest bucket name.
169	
170	**Sample output (raw bytes):**
171	
172	```
173	wheels-v6            123456789
174	sdists-v9            45678901
175	flat-index-v2        1024
176	git-v0               0
177	interpreter-v4       5120
178	simple-v21           15360000
179	archive-v0           204800000
180	builds-v0            0
181	environments-v2      0
182	binaries-v0          0
183	------------------------------
184	Total                389985934
185	```
186	
187	**Sample output (`--human`):**
188	
189	```
190	wheels-v6            117.7 MiB
191	sdists-v9            43.5 MiB
192	flat-index-v2        1.0 KiB
193	git-v0               0.0 B
194	interpreter-v4       5.0 KiB
195	simple-v21           14.6 MiB
196	archive-v0           195.3 MiB
197	builds-v0            0.0 B
198	environments-v2      0.0 B
199	binaries-v0          0.0 B
200	------------------------------
201	Total                371.2 MiB
202	```
203	
204	---
205	
206	### 7. `crates/uv/tests/it/cache_stats.rs` (new file)
207	
208	Three test cases following the exact same pattern as `crates/uv/tests/it/cache_size.rs`:
209	
210	1. **`cache_stats_empty_raw`** — clean cache, run `uv cache stats --preview`, snapshot all-zero output.
211	2. **`cache_stats_with_packages_raw`** — install `iniconfig`, run `uv cache stats --preview`, use `with_filtered_cache_size().filters()` to replace variable numeric sizes with `[SIZE]`.
212	3. **`cache_stats_with_packages_human`** — install `iniconfig`, run `uv cache stats --preview --human`, same filtering.
213	
214	```rust
215	use assert_cmd::assert::OutputAssertExt;
216	use uv_test::uv_snapshot;
217	
218	#[test]
219	fn cache_stats_empty_raw() {
220	    let context = uv_test::test_context!("3.12");
221	    context.clean().assert().success();
222	    uv_snapshot!(context.cache_stats().arg("--preview"), @"...");
223	}
224	
225	#[test]
226	fn cache_stats_with_packages_raw() {
227	    let context = uv_test::test_context!("3.12");
228	    context.pip_install().arg("iniconfig").assert().success();
229	    uv_snapshot!(context.with_filtered_cache_size().filters(),
230	        context.cache_stats().arg("--preview"), @"...");
231	}
232	
233	#[test]
234	fn cache_stats_with_packages_human() {
235	    let context = uv_test::test_context!("3.12");
236	    context.pip_install().arg("iniconfig").assert().success();
237	    uv_snapshot!(context.with_filtered_cache_size().filters(),
238	        context.cache_stats().arg("--preview").arg("--human"), @"...");
239	}
240	```
241	
242	Note: `context.cache_stats()` needs to be added to the test context (or use `context.command()` to invoke `uv cache stats`). Check how `context.cache_size()` is implemented in the test framework (`uv_test` crate) to follow the same pattern exactly.
243	
244	---

249	| ----------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------- |

251	| `human_readable_bytes`  | `crates/uv-console/src/lib.rs`, re-exported at `commands/mod.rs:65` | Format bytes as "1.2 GiB"        |
252	| `CacheBucket::iter()`   | `crates/uv-cache/src/lib.rs:1316`                                   | Iterate all active cache buckets |
253	| `Cache::bucket()`       | `crates/uv-cache/src/lib.rs:281`                                    | Get path to a bucket directory   |
254	| `CacheBucket` `Display` | `crates/uv-cache/src/lib.rs:1334`                                   | Get versioned bucket name string |
255	| `PreviewFeature`        | `crates/uv-preview/src/lib.rs:168`                                  | Feature-gate with warning        |
256	
257	---

259	## Verification
260	
261	1. **Smoke test (empty cache):**
262	
263	   ```
264	   uv cache clean && uv cache stats --preview
265	   ```
266	
267	   Should print all zeros.

269	2. **Smoke test (with data):**

275	   ```

278

283	   ```

294	
295	5. **Help text:**
296	   ```

298	   ```
299	   Should show the `--human` / `-H` flag.

file.content:
## Plan: `uv cache stats`

Here's the full implementation plan — **8 file changes, zero new dependencies**.

### Files to modify (6):

1. **`crates/uv-preview/src/lib.rs`** — Add `CacheStats = 1 << 28` to `PreviewFeature` enum, plus matching entries in `as_str()` and `FromStr`

2. **`crates/uv-cli/src/lib.rs`** — Add `Stats(StatsArgs)` variant to `CacheCommand` + a `StatsArgs` struct (mirrors `SizeArgs` exactly with the same `--human / -H / --human-readable` flag)

3. **`crates/uv/src/commands/mod.rs`** — Add `pub(crate) use cache_stats::cache_stats;` and `mod cache_stats;`

4. **`crates/uv/src/lib.rs`** — Add `CacheCommand::Stats` dispatch arm after the `Size` arm (line 1181)

5. **`crates/uv-test/src/lib.rs`** — Add `cache_stats()` command helper and `with_filtered_cache_stats()` filter (for snapshot test normalization) to `TestContext`

6. **`crates/uv/tests/it/main.rs`** — Add `mod cache_stats;` alongside `mod cache_size;`

### Files to create (2):

7. **`crates/uv/src/commands/cache_stats.rs`** — Core logic: iterate `CacheBucket::iter()` (10 buckets), measure each with `diskus::DiskUsage`, print a 20-char left-padded table, then `---` separator and `Total` row

8. **`crates/uv/tests/it/cache_stats.rs`** — 3 integration tests (empty cache all-zeros, raw bytes with packages, human-readable with packages)

### Key design decisions:
- Reuses `CacheBucket::iter()` — the same 10 buckets `cache size` and `cache clean` use (`Python` excluded, consistent with existing code)
- Per-bucket measurement uses `diskus::DiskUsage` (already a dependency via `cache_size.rs`) on each bucket path independently
- `CacheBucket`'s `Display` impl provides versioned directory names (`wheels-v6`, `sdists-v9`, etc.)
- `--human` / `-H` / `--human-readable` flags and `human_readable_bytes()` formatting identical to `uv cache size`
- Gated behind new `PreviewFeature::CacheStats` (`--preview-features cache-stats`) with identical experimental warning

880	    /// Clear the cache, removing all entries or those linked to specific packages.

884	    Prune(PruneArgs),
885	    /// Show the cache directory.

888	    /// `%LOCALAPPDATA%\uv\cache` on Windows.
889	    ///
890	    /// When `--no-cache` is used, the cache is stored in a temporary directory and discarded when
891	    /// the process exits.
892	    ///
893	    /// An alternative cache directory may be specified via the `cache-dir` setting, the
894	    /// `--cache-dir` option, or the `$UV_CACHE_DIR` environment variable.
895	    ///
896	    /// Note that it is important for performance for the cache directory to be located on the same

use assert_cmd::assert::OutputAssertExt;

use uv_test::uv_snapshot;

/// Test that `cache stats` returns all-zeros for an empty cache (raw output).
#[test]
fn cache_stats_empty_raw() {
    let context = uv_test::test_context!("3.12");

    // Clean cache first to ensure truly empty state.
    context.clean().assert().success();

    uv_snapshot!(context.cache_stats().arg("--preview"), @"
    success: true
    exit_code: 0
    ----- stdout -----
    wheels-v6             0
    sdists-v9             0
    flat-index-v2         0
    git-v0                0
    interpreter-v4        0
    simple-v21            0
    archive-v0            0
    builds-v0             0
    environments-v2       0
    binaries-v0           0
    ---
    Total                 0

    ----- stderr -----
    ");
}

/// Test that `cache stats` shows raw bytes per bucket after installing packages.
#[test]
fn cache_stats_with_packages_raw() {
    let context = uv_test::test_context!("3.12");

    // Install a requirement to populate the cache.
    context.pip_install().arg("iniconfig").assert().success();

    uv_snapshot!(context.with_filtered_cache_stats().filters(), context.cache_stats().arg("--preview"), @"
    success: true
    exit_code: 0
    ----- stdout -----
    wheels-v6             [SIZE]
    sdists-v9             [SIZE]
    flat-index-v2         [SIZE]
    git-v0                [SIZE]
    interpreter-v4        [SIZE]
    simple-v21            [SIZE]
    archive-v0            [SIZE]
    builds-v0             [SIZE]
    environments-v2       [SIZE]
    binaries-v0           [SIZE]
    ---
    Total                 [SIZE]

    ----- stderr -----
    ");
}

/// Test that `cache stats --human` shows human-readable sizes per bucket after installing packages.
#[test]
fn cache_stats_with_packages_human() {
    let context = uv_test::test_context!("3.12");

    // Install a requirement to populate the cache.
    context.pip_install().arg("iniconfig").assert().success();

    uv_snapshot!(context.with_filtered_cache_stats().filters(), context.cache_stats().arg("--preview").arg("--human"), @"
    success: true
    exit_code: 0
    ----- stdout -----
    wheels-v6             [SIZE]
    sdists-v9             [SIZE]
    flat-index-v2         [SIZE]
    git-v0                [SIZE]
    interpreter-v4        [SIZE]
    simple-v21            [SIZE]
    archive-v0            [SIZE]
    builds-v0             [SIZE]
    environments-v2       [SIZE]
    binaries-v0           [SIZE]
    ---
    Total                 [SIZE]

    ----- stderr -----
    ");
}
